title: Photos: Here are the best scenes from the Germany-Paraguay World Cup matchup at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Paraguay defeated Germany in the World Cup Round of 32 in Foxborough after a penalty shootout.
José Canale scored the decisive penalty kick, knocking Germany out of the tournament.
Miguel Almirón of Atlanta United played a key role in Paraguay's victory at 'Boston Stadium.'

title: Search continues for missing teen and his two dogs near the Connecticut border
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 16-year-old from Monson and his two dogs went missing on Tuesday, last seen heading towards Connecticut.
Search efforts involve ground and aerial resources, including State Police helicopters, K-9 units, and Connecticut officials.
Residents near Moulton Hill and Childs Road were asked to check properties but avoid conducting independent searches.

title: Educators balk as Merrimack, Suffolk plan to roll out 3-year bachelor’s degrees
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Merrimack College and Suffolk University to launch three-year bachelor's degree programs in fall 2027.
Pilot programs approved by Massachusetts Board of Higher Education aim to cut costs with 94-96 credit degrees.
Massachusetts Teachers Association raises concerns over potential inequities and erosion of academic quality.

title: HGTV’s Taniya Nayak talks designing a Bruin’s home and new Boston restaurants
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Taniya Nayak, an HGTV host and Massachusetts native, is designing Patrice Bergeron's Newton home and several new Boston restaurants.
Nayak received an honorary doctorate from Boston Architectural College and has a Boston-based design company for luxury homes.
Nayak co-owns Massachusetts and Rhode Island restaurants, collaborating on new projects like Buitoni Spaghetti Bar in Kenmore Square.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (July 1)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A Colonial home in Acton at 709 Main St., built in 2024, sold for $1,550,000 on a 41,279-square-foot lot.
In Beverly, a Cape Cod style home at 6 Fern St., built in 1995, sold for $1,200,000 with four bedrooms.
Arlington's 68 Dothan St., a raised ranch built in 1987, with three bedrooms, sold for $1,345,000 on a 13,852-square-foot lot.

title: Robert Parish: Talk of Celtics trading Jaylen Brown is ‘disturbing’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Robert Parish criticized potential Celtics trade of Jaylen Brown, calling it a serious miscalculation by management.
Brad Stevens acknowledged potential for roster changes following Celtics' early playoff exit against the Sixers.
Celtics' trade negotiations for Brown reportedly stalled; team seeks four first-round picks among other assets.

title: State Police sergeant, former lieutenant under criminal investigation for alleged coverup in wake of fatal crash, court documents reveal
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts State Police Sgt. Mark Delaney and former Lt. Anthony DeLucia are under investigation for alleged case misconduct.
Sgt. Scott Quigley faced motor vehicle homicide charges after a 2023 crash allegedly involving driving under the influence.
Investigation delays have affected the murder trial of the Phan brothers, originally linked to a 2020 Lowell case.

title: 5 players Bruins should target in free agency on Wednesday
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins have $7.7 million in cap space to enhance their roster in free agency starting Wednesday.
Ryan Shea, a mobile defenseman with the Penguins, is a potential signing to bolster the Bruins' blue line.
For added scoring, speedy winger Matias Maccelli from the Maple Leafs could be a budget-friendly forward option.

title: Watch: Red Sox, Nationals brawl after Willson Contreras charges mound
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox's Willson Contreras charged the mound after a strikeout dispute with Nationals pitcher Cade Cavalli at Fenway Park.
Three players, including Contreras, were ejected following the brawl that erupted during the fourth inning of the game.
Interim manager Chad Tracy was also ejected for arguing that Cavalli should have been penalized for his remark towards Contreras.

title: Person shot and killed, suspect also dead after incident that closed I-495 in Lowell for hours, officials say
author: Darin Zullo and Samantha Genzer
bullets: A suspect and a victim both died following a shooting and standoff on I-495 in Lowell.
The incident closed I-495 in both directions for several hours during Tuesday's rush hour.
Authorities confirmed the suspect and victim were acquainted; investigation into the incident is ongoing.
